info.title: Features Service API
info.description:
Manages feature definitions and computes output stats.

## Key Concepts

**Features** define what campaigns can do: inputs (form), outputs (metrics), charts (funnel/breakdown), and entities (detail tabs). Features are **global** — shared across all orgs. They are template definitions, not per-org resources.

## Feature Definition

```typescript
{
  inputs:   [{ key, label, type, ... }],           // campaign creation form
  outputs:  [{ key, displayOrder }],                // metrics (validated against registry)
  charts:   [{ type: "funnel-bar", steps: [...] }], // visualizations
  entities: ["leads", "companies", "emails"],       // campaign detail tabs
}
```

## Fork-on-Write (Immutability)

Features are **immutable** once created. No endpoint mutates inputs or outputs in place.

`PUT /features/{slug}` uses **fork-on-write** semantics:

| Scenario | Behavior | HTTP |
|----------|----------|------|
| Only metadata changes (description, icon, charts…) | Update in-place | `200` |
| Inputs or outputs change (different signature) | Create new feature (fork), deprecate original | `201` |
| New signature already exists elsewhere | Conflict | `409` |

On fork:
- A new **dynasty** is created with an auto-generated codename (e.g. `Sales Cold Email` → `Sales Cold Email Sophia`)
- `dynastyName`/`dynastySlug` are new and unique, `version` resets to 1
- `forkedFrom` on the new feature points to the original's ID
- `upgradedTo` on the original points to the new feature's ID
- The original's `status` is set to `deprecated`

On upgrade (same dynasty, signature changed via `PUT /features` batch upsert):
- `dynastyName`/`dynastySlug` are preserved, `version` is incremented
- `name` gets version suffix (e.g. `Sales Cold Email v2`), `slug` gets `-v2`

This is aligned with workflow-service's dynasty model.

## Dynasty Model

Features use a **dynasty model** for versioning, aligned with workflow-service:

| Field | Purpose | Changes on upgrade? | Changes on fork? | Example |
|-------|---------|--------------------|-----------------|---------|
| `dynastyName` | Stable dynasty name for UI | **No** | **Yes** — new codename | `Sales Cold Email Sophia` |
| `dynastySlug` | Stable dynasty slug | **No** | **Yes** | `sales-cold-email-sophia` |
| `version` | Version within dynasty | **Yes** — incremented | Reset to 1 | `2` |
| `name` | Versioned unique name | **Yes** | **Yes** | `Sales Cold Email Sophia v2` |
| `slug` | Versioned unique slug | **Yes** | **Yes** | `sales-cold-email-sophia-v2` |

**Always use `dynastyName` for UI display.** The `name`/`slug` are for internal routing.

On fork, a **codename** is auto-generated (e.g. 'Sophia', 'Berlin') to create a unique dynasty name.

Use `GET /features/dynasty?slug=...` to resolve the stable dynasty identity from any versioned slug.

## Lineage Chain

Each feature can have:
- `forkedFrom` (uuid | null) — the parent feature this was forked from
- `upgradedTo` (uuid | null) — if deprecated, the replacement feature

To traverse the full lineage of a feature, follow `forkedFrom` upward (ancestors) and `upgradedTo` downward (descendants). Campaigns and workflows keep their original `featureSlug` — they are **never** migrated on fork. This preserves full audit history of which feature version produced which results.

## Stats Aggregation Across the Chain

When you request stats for a feature (`GET /features/{slug}/stats`), the service automatically resolves the **full upgrade chain** using BFS (breadth-first search) on a predecessor map. This handles:

- **Linear chains:** A v1 → A v2 → A v3 (simple upgrade sequence)
- **Convergence:** Two dynasties independently produce the same signature — both lineages converge on one active feature. BFS traverses all predecessor branches.

```
Dynasty A: v1 (deprecated) → A v2 (active, sig:DEF)
                                    ↑
Dynasty B: v1 (deprecated) ─────────┘  (B upgraded to same sig:DEF)
```
Querying stats for A v2 aggregates data from A v1, B v1, and A v2.

This means:
- Old campaigns/workflows still referencing a deprecated slug are included
- You always get the complete picture regardless of which slug in the chain you query
- No data is lost on fork or convergence

## Stats Registry

`GET /stats/registry` — the finite universe of known stats keys. Each key has a label and type (count, rate, currency). Features reference these keys in outputs and charts.

## Stats Computation

Stats endpoints compute values by calling downstream services (email-gateway, runs-service, outlets-service) and returning aggregated results. Keys are either **raw** (fetched from a source) or **derived** (computed as a ratio of two raw keys, e.g. `replyRate = emailsReplied / emailsSent`).

## Registration (Cold Start)

`PUT /features` — idempotent batch upsert, safe to call on every boot. Uses signature-based deduplication: same signature → update metadata, different signature → create or auto-version. This is the equivalent of workflow-service's `PUT /workflows/upgrade`.

slug.description: Globally unique versioned slug (e.g. 'sales-cold-email-sophia-v2'). Composed: dynasty_slug + version suffix.

name.description: Globally unique versioned name (e.g. 'Sales Cold Email Sophia v2'). Composed: dynasty_name + version suffix.

dynastyName.description: Stable name across all versions of this dynasty (e.g. 'Sales Cold Email Sophia'). Use for UI display.

dynastySlug.description: Stable slug across all versions of this dynasty (e.g. 'sales-cold-email-sophia').

version.description: Version number within the dynasty (1-based). v1 has no suffix in name/slug.

icon.description: Lucide icon name (e.g. 'envelope', 'globe', 'megaphone'). Use as <LucideIcon name={icon} /> or look up at lucide.dev/icons.

signature.description: Deterministic hash of sorted input+output keys — used for idempotent upsert and convergence detection

inputs.description: Input fields for the campaign creation form.

outputs.description: Output metrics — stats keys from the registry with display config. Use GET /stats/registry for labels and types.

charts.description: Chart definitions (funnel-bar, breakdown-bar). At least one of each required.

entities.description: Entity types shown in campaign detail sidebar (e.g. ['leads', 'companies', 'emails'])

forkedFrom.description: If this feature was forked from another, the ID of the original.

upgradedTo.description: If deprecated, the ID of the replacement feature.

brandId.description: Brand that was used for pre-fill

prefilled.description: Map of input key → flattened text value (or null if extraction failed).

PrefillTextResponse.description: Pre-filled values as flat strings, ready for form inputs or workflow inputMapping

brandId.description: Brand that was used for pre-fill

value.description: Extracted value — can be a string, array, or object depending on the field

cached.description: Whether the value was served from cache

sourceUrls.description: URLs from the brand's website used to extract this value

prefilled.description: Map of input key → full extraction result.

PrefillFullResponse.description: Pre-filled values with metadata (cache status, source URLs)

feature_dynasty_name.description: Stable dynasty name (unversioned), e.g. 'Sales Cold Email Sophia'

slugs.description: All versioned slugs in the dynasty, sorted by version ascending

put.description:
Idempotent — safe to call on every cold start. Platform-only (X-API-Key auth).

Uses a **signature** (SHA-256 hash of sorted input+output keys) to detect duplicates:
- **Same signature** → upsert metadata in-place (labels, descriptions, charts, entities). No version/dynasty change.
- **Same dynasty, different signature** → upgrade: deprecate old feature, create new version in the same dynasty (same `dynastyName`/`dynastySlug`, incremented `version`).
- **New name** → create new dynasty (`version: 1`, no version suffix in slug/name).

The `name` field in the request body becomes the `dynastyName` (stable across versions). The versioned `name` and `slug` are auto-computed from `dynastyName` + `version`.

**Example upgrade chain:**
```
Boot 1: name='Sales Cold Email', inputs=[a,b] → creates 'sales-cold-email' v1
Boot 2: name='Sales Cold Email', inputs=[a,b] → same signature, metadata update only
Boot 3: name='Sales Cold Email', inputs=[a,b,c] → new signature, creates 'sales-cold-email-v2', deprecates v1
```

This is the equivalent of workflow-service's `PUT /workflows/upgrade`.

post.description:
Creates a brand-new feature and a new dynasty. Slug is auto-generated from name if not provided. Returns 409 if a feature with the same signature already exists.

The `name` field becomes the `dynastyName`. If a dynasty with the same name already exists, a codename is auto-generated to create a unique dynasty (e.g. 'Sales Cold Email' → 'Sales Cold Email Sophia').

**Example response (new dynasty, no collision):**
```json
{ "feature": { "dynastyName": "Sales Cold Email", "dynastySlug": "sales-cold-email", "version": 1, "slug": "sales-cold-email", "name": "Sales Cold Email" } }
```

**Example response (name collision, auto-generated codename):**
```json
{ "feature": { "dynastyName": "Sales Cold Email Sophia", "dynastySlug": "sales-cold-email-sophia", "version": 1, "slug": "sales-cold-email-sophia", "name": "Sales Cold Email Sophia" } }
```

get.description:
Returns features filtered by query params. Defaults to `status=active` — deprecated features (replaced by a fork) are excluded unless explicitly requested.

Use `status=deprecated` to see the full history, or omit the status filter to see all.

put.description:
**Fork-on-write** — features are immutable. This endpoint decides automatically:

**Same signature (metadata-only change) → 200:**
Fields like description, icon, category, charts, entities, displayOrder can be updated in place because they don't affect the feature's identity (inputs + outputs). Even if you send `inputs` or `outputs` in the body, if the resulting signature is unchanged, it's treated as metadata-only.

**Different signature (inputs/outputs changed) → 201 (fork):**
A **new dynasty** is created with an auto-generated codename:
- New `dynastyName` = original's base name + codename (e.g. `Sales Cold Email Sophia`)
- `version: 1`, `forkedFrom` pointing to the original
- All other fields merged from the update body + the original

The original is deprecated (`status: deprecated`, `upgradedTo` set). Existing campaigns/workflows referencing the old slug are **not** migrated — stats aggregation traverses the full lineage chain automatically.

**Convergence (different signature matches an existing feature) → 200:**
If the new signature already exists on another feature, no new record is created. The current feature is deprecated and its `upgradedTo` points to the existing feature with that signature. Both lineages converge on a single active feature. Stats BFS traverses all predecessor branches.

**Example fork:**
```
PUT /features/sales-cold-email { inputs: [changed] }
→ 201: new feature 'Sales Cold Email Sophia' (slug: sales-cold-email-sophia)
  original 'sales-cold-email' → deprecated, upgradedTo → sophia
```

**Example convergence:**
```
Dynasty A: v1 (sig:abc) → v2 (sig:DEF, active)
Dynasty B: v1 (sig:xyz) → PUT with new inputs → sig:DEF already exists!
→ B v1 deprecated, upgradedTo → A v2. Both lineages converge.
```

get.summary: Resolve dynasty identity from a versioned feature slug
get.description:
Returns the stable, unversioned dynasty identifiers for a given feature slug. Used by workflow-service to compose workflow names.

Example: `?slug=sales-cold-email-sophia-v2` → `{ feature_dynasty_name: 'Sales Cold Email Sophia', feature_dynasty_slug: 'sales-cold-email-sophia' }`

Works for both active and deprecated features.

schema.description: The versioned feature slug to resolve

parameters.description: The versioned feature slug to resolve
parameters.name: slug

schema.description: Run ID for tracking and billing

parameters.description: Run ID for tracking and billing
parameters.name: x-run-id

feature_dynasty_name.description: Stable dynasty name (unversioned), e.g. 'Sales Cold Email Sophia'

get.summary: List all versioned slugs for a dynasty
get.description:
Returns all feature slugs (active + deprecated) belonging to the given dynasty slug. Useful for downstream services that store versioned feature slugs and need to aggregate stats across all versions of a dynasty (e.g. `WHERE feature_slug IN (...)`).

Example: `/features/by-dynasty/sales-cold-email-sophia/slugs` → `{ slugs: ['sales-cold-email-sophia', 'sales-cold-email-sophia-v2', 'sales-cold-email-sophia-v3'] }`

parameters.description: Run ID for tracking and billing
parameters.name: x-run-id

slugs.description: All versioned slugs in the dynasty, sorted by version ascending

404.description: No features found for this dynasty slug

get.description: Returns the complete dictionary of known stats keys with their label and type. Used by the front-end to format and label output columns dynamically.

schema.description: Run ID for tracking and billing

parameters.description: Run ID for tracking and billing
parameters.name: x-run-id

get.summary: Get computed stats for a feature
get.description:
Returns computed stats for a feature's outputs and charts. Optionally grouped by workflowSlug, brandId, or campaignId. System stats (cost, runs, campaigns, dates) are always included.

**Lineage aggregation:** Stats are automatically aggregated across the full upgrade chain (deprecated ancestors + active descendants). If a feature was forked, querying any slug in the chain returns the combined stats. This ensures no data is lost when features evolve.

Stats keys are either **raw** (fetched from email-gateway, runs-service, or outlets-service) or **derived** (computed as a ratio, e.g. `replyRate = emailsReplied / emailsSent`). Use `GET /stats/registry` to discover available keys, their labels, and types.

schema.description: Run ID for tracking and billing

parameters.description: Run ID for tracking and billing
parameters.name: x-run-id

get.description:
Cross-feature stats endpoint for performance dashboards and org overview. Supports groupBy: featureSlug, workflowSlug, brandId, campaignId.

Only active features are included in the computation. Stats from deprecated features are aggregated into their active successor via the lineage chain.

schema.description: Comma-separated dimensions: featureSlug, workflowSlug, brandId, campaignId

parameters.description: Comma-separated dimensions: featureSlug, workflowSlug, brandId, campaignId
parameters.name: groupBy

schema.description: Run ID for tracking and billing

parameters.description: Run ID for tracking and billing
parameters.name: x-run-id
